transformation_type: alcohol dehydration via xanthate pyrolysis / syn elimination
mechanism_type: thermal six-membered syn elimination of xanthate esters
common_conditions: alcohol to xanthate conversion then high-temperature pyrolysis; syn elimination
description_short: Thermal syn elimination of xanthate esters derived from alcohols to furnish alkenes under rearrangement-free conditions.

mechanism_type: dihalocarbene insertion followed by ring expansion and dehydrohalogenation
common_conditions: strongly basic carbene-generation conditions; ring expansion of pyrroles or indoles
description_short: Ring expansion of pyrroles or indoles with dihalocarbenes to give 3-halopyridines, 3-haloquinolines, and related azines.

transformation_type: ester enolate acylation to beta-keto esters / Dieckmann cyclization
mechanism_type: nucleophilic acyl substitution by ester enolates
common_conditions: matching alkoxide or strong base; acidic workup after beta-keto ester formation
synonym_names: Claisen Condensation|Claisen Reaction|mixed Claisen condensation|Dieckmann condensation
description_short: Base-mediated condensation of esters through ester enolate acylation to give beta-keto esters, crossed products, or cyclic Dieckmann products.

transformation_type: allyl ether to allyl carbonyl or allyl phenol [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
key_reagents_clue: allyl vinyl ether|allyl aryl ether|heat|Lewis acid variants
common_conditions: thermal rearrangement or Lewis-acid-assisted rearrangement of allyl ethers
description_short: Thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allyl vinyl and allyl aryl ethers to gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds or allyl phenols.

transformation_type: allylic ester O-silyl ketene acetal rearrangement to gamma,delta-unsaturated acids
mechanism_type: ester-enolate-derived [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement via O-silyl ketene acetals
common_conditions: low-temperature enolate generation and silylation followed by warming and hydrolysis
description_short: Stereocontrolled [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic ester enolates trapped as O-silyl ketene acetals to gamma,delta-unsaturated carboxylic acids.
